Can I do drop sets in Old School mode for Cable Fly?

Old School is a non-adaptive mode — when you set 70kg for Cable Fly, the machine delivers exactly that weight and doesn't adjust mid-rep based on your movement speed. You can do drop sets manually — just change the weight between sets. Since Old School is non-adaptive, the weight stays exactly where you set it.

Is Eccentric Only at 80kg on Bench Press good for strength or hypertrophy?

Eccentric Only adds load specifically to the lowering phase of Bench Press. When you set 80kg, the eccentric phase will feel heavier than the concentric — that's the point. The concentric (lifting) phase is lighter so you can handle the heavier eccentric. Focus on controlling the lowering phase on Bench Press — that's where the training stimulus is. Don't just let the weight drop. A 3-5 second eccentric is a good starting point.

Is Pump mode safe for heavy Romanian Deadlift at 50lbs?

Pump is an adaptive mode that loads you based on pace. At 50lbs on Romanian Deadlift, if you move too slowly the machine deloads you, and if you move fast enough it loads toward the selected resistance. Pump works well for compound movements like Romanian Deadlift at moderate load. Just keep the pace up and don't stall at any point in the rep.

Can I do 40 sets of Lat Pulldown per week in Eccentric Only mode?

That level of training volume/frequency on Lat Pulldown is very likely to lead to diminishing returns and increased injury risk. Research consistently shows that beyond a certain threshold, more is not better — you get worse results, not better. A sustainable approach: 5 sessions per week, 12-18 working sets per muscle group, with 2 rest days. For Lat Pulldown specifically, 2-3 direct sessions per week is usually the sweet spot. If you want to be active daily, alternate hard training with light recovery work (mobility, easy TUT at low load).

I've had popping in my hip for 5 weeks during Overhead Press. What exercises can I still do?

Persistent popping in your hip during Overhead Press is a signal to stop that movement and get it evaluated by a medical professional — physio or sports medicine. I can't diagnose what's causing the popping. After 5+ weeks, self-managing isnt working — see a professional. In the meantime, focus on pain-free alternatives: upper-body work, knee-dominant exercises if pain-free, and core stability. Reduce load significantly on anything that involves the hip, and use controlled tempo (TUT mode is good for this). Don't rely on the machine's deload feature as a substitute for clinical evaluation.

My TUT sets on Bicep Curl feel too easy at 50lbs. Should I speed up or add weight?

Time Under Tension is an adaptive mode designed for slow, controlled movement on exercises like Bicep Curl. The machine adjusts load at sticking points to keep tension consistent throughout the rep. If TUT feels too easy at 50lbs, try adding weight rather than speeding up. Moving too fast in TUT can trigger a deload because the mode is specifically designed for slow, controlled work.
